content.problemStatement:
You are given a **binary** string SSS, of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on the string:


Select an index iii (1<i<N)(1\lt i\lt N)(1<i<N) and set Si=Si−1⊕Si+1S_i = S_{i-1}\oplus S_{i+1}Si​=Si−1​⊕Si+1​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise xor operation.
Find the string with the **maximum** number of 111's you can obtain, after applying this operation several (possibly zero) times.

If there are multiple strings that have the maximum number of 111's, find the **lexicographically smallest** one among them.

String XXX is lexicographically smaller than string YYY, if there exists an index iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​ and Xj=Yj,∀jX_j = Y_j, \forall jXj​=Yj​,∀j such that 1≤j<i1 \leq j \lt i1≤j<i.

That is, XXX is smaller than YYY at the first character where they differ.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the string.
The second line of each test case consists of a binary string SSS, of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **lexicographically smallest** string, with **maximum** number of 111s, you can obtain after applying the operation any (possibly zero) number of times.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are already 333 ones in the string. Thus, we do not need to perform any operation.
Test case 222: The only operation we can perform is choosing i=2i = 2i=2. On making the operation, S2S_2S2​ becomes S1⊕S3=0⊕0=0S_1\oplus S_3 = 0\oplus 0 = 0S1​⊕S3​=0⊕0=0 and the string becomes 000000000.
Thus, it is optimal to perform no operation so that the number of ones remains 111 which is the maximum possible.
Test case 333: Choose i=2i = 2i=2, to make S2=S1⊕S3=0⊕1=1S_2 = S_1\oplus S_3 = 0\oplus 1 = 1S2​=S1​⊕S3​=0⊕1=1. The string becomes 011001100110, consisting of 222 ones.
It can be shown that this is the maximum possible number of ones.
